hero.subtitle: UniMind is the AI infrastructure for tutoring institutions — plug in and unlock three core capabilities: AI question generation, Memorix adaptive review, and the Knowledge Workbench. Whether you teach finance, law, medicine, CPA, or grad exam prep, input your knowledge points and AI generates the questions.
hero.footnote: No credit card required · 14-day full-feature trial · All subjects

stats.label: Institutions Onboarded
stats.desc: From solo teachers to chain brands

pain.label: The Problem
pain.title: The Three Biggest Hidden Costs of Tutoring Institutions
pain.subtitle: Not rent. Not acquisition. It's neglected teaching efficiency, review effectiveness, and data blind spots.

items.title: Question Creation is Slow
items.desc: A good question = topic selection + stem writing + options + explanation. 30 minutes per question, a full day per exam.

items.title: Review is Dumb
items.desc: Every student drills the same set. Top students waste time on what they know; weak students struggle blindly with new material.

items.title: Data is Blind
items.desc: You check Excel at month-end for accuracy rates. Which student keeps failing which topic? You don't know — until the exam is over.

features.label: Core Features
features.title: Three Core Capabilities
features.subtitle: AI question engine produces content, Memorix manages review rhythm, Knowledge Workbench visualizes mastery — a complete loop from content production to learning delivery.

items.title: AI Question Generation
items.subtitle: ARC Adversarial Pipeline · Ready to Use
items.desc: Our multi-agent adversarial architecture — Author, Reviewer, and Classifier — iterates on every question. Substandard generations are automatically rejected and retried until the quality score exceeds the threshold. Compared to single LLM calls, the adversarial pipeline boosts question usability from ~60% to 85%+. All question types supported — multiple choice, calculation, case analysis — across all subjects. Finance, law, medicine, CPA — AI generates in each discipline's academic style.
points: Author → Reviewer → Classifier adversarial pipeline, up to 3 iterations
All subjects, all question types, discipline-specific style

items.title: Memorix Adaptive Review
items.subtitle: Research-grade forgetting models, precise review before you forget
items.desc: Memorix is our proprietary memory scheduling algorithm. Unlike traditional spaced repetition, it models forgetting with a Weibull distribution — more accurately capturing the "fast then slow" human memory decay pattern. After each answer, the algorithm updates 20 personalized parameters via online SGD with Nesterov Momentum, calibrated with Brier scores. On our Finance 431 dataset (500+ users, 120K review logs), Memorix reduced prediction RMSE by 13.7% vs FSRS v4.5 and improved knowledge retention by 9.2%.
points: Weibull forgetting model — more accurate than traditional power-law models
Online SGD + Nesterov — every answer evolves your memory profile
Brier Score calibration — mathematically guaranteed forgetting predictions
20-dimension personal parameters — individual memory portrait, not group average

items.title: Knowledge Workbench
items.subtitle: Visual knowledge graph, mastery at a glance
items.desc: Each knowledge point appears as a colored node on the interactive graph — green for mastered, red for weak, gray for untouched. Click any node to see related questions, mistake records, and course resources. Students visualize their knowledge structure; teachers pinpoint class weaknesses instantly.
points: Color-coded by mastery, weak points auto-highlighted
One-click jump to questions, mistakes, and courses

how.label: How It Works
how.title: Three Steps, Live in 10 Minutes
how.subtitle: No tech team needed. No AI engineers. Register → Generate → Students practice. That's it.

steps.title: Choose Subject
steps.desc: Register your institution, choose a subject. We pre-built frameworks for 10+ disciplines — grad exams, certifications, civil service — with custom support.

steps.title: AI Generates
steps.desc: Enter the AI generation center, pick knowledge points and question types. The three-agent pipeline auto-generates. Review and approve with one click.

steps.title: Students Practice + Analytics
steps.desc: Students practice via a shared link — no app download needed. Auto-grading after each answer. The Knowledge Workbench updates every student's mastery map in real time.

subjects.label: All Subjects
subjects.title: Not Limited to Any One Subject
subjects.subtitle: UniMind's AI engine is an expert at "how to write a good question" — give it any subject's knowledge points, and it generates questions in that discipline's style.
subjects.footer: Don't see your subject? Customize your knowledge tree — AI adapts instantly.

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.title: Choose Your Plan
pricing.subtitle: All plans include a 14-day free trial. No credit card required.
pricing.footer: First 20 paying customers lock in early-adopter pricing for life.

items.q: What are the limits of the free plan?
items.a: The Free plan supports 30 students and 50 AI generations per month. You can experience the full loop — generate → practice → analytics — enough to validate the product with a small class. Upgrade to Solo to remove all limits.

We use a three-agent adversarial mechanism — one generates, one reviews, one classifies. Substandard questions are automatically rejected and retried. We recommend your teaching staff do a final review — AI saves 90% of drafting time; the last 10% still needs your professional judgment.

The platform is subject-agnostic. We pre-built knowledge frameworks for grad entrance exams (Finance, Law, Medicine, CS, etc.), professional certifications (CPA, CFA, Bar, Teaching, etc.), and civil service exams. You can also customize your own subjects and knowledge trees.

items.q: How do students use it? Do they need an app?
items.a: No. Students open a link you share and start practicing. We provide a mobile web experience — zero friction for students. No app download required.

items.q: What happens to my data after the trial ends?
items.a: Everything stays. All your questions, student data, and learning records are preserved. Renew to restore full access. Without renewal, data is read-only — you lose nothing.
Yes. Both monthly and annual billing are supported. Annual billing includes a 23-33% discount. We recommend starting monthly for a deep trial, then switching to annual once you're confident.

items.q: How is this different from generic exam tools?
items.a: Generic exam tools "move paper tests online." UniMind is "AI that produces and optimizes your teaching content" — questions are AI-generated, review is algorithm-personalized, analytics are real-time. We don't solve "how to test" — we solve "how to teach and how to learn."

items.q: I'm an individual teacher. Which plan should I choose?
items.a: Start with the Free plan. 30 students and 50 AI generations per month cover daily needs. Upgrade to Solo (¥299/mo, ¥199/mo annual) when you exceed 30 students or need more generations.

cta.title: Ready to bring AI to your institution?
cta.subtitle: 14-day full-feature free trial. No card, no charge. All subjects. See how much time AI can save your institution on question creation.
